rule of thirds, subject on left/right third intersection, eyes on upper third line
golden ratio phi grid, golden spiral leading to face, 1.618 placement
leading lines (road·rail·desk edge·crowd) converging toward subject
one-point perspective, central vanishing point down a corridor
dynamic diagonal lines, stable triangular grouping of figures
S-curve composition winding through the frame
frame within a frame (doorway·window·arch) around the subject
foreground occlusion, shoulders/blur object framing the subject
foreground/midground/background separation, atmospheric depth
perfectly symmetrical centered composition, Wes-Anderson style
off-balance asymmetry, subject pushed to one edge, tension in empty side
radial composition, elements radiating from a central point
dominant negative space around a small subject
correct headroom, slight space above the head (not cramped, not floating)
lead room / nose room, space ahead of gaze or motion direction
eyeline on upper-third, gaze directed across the open space
fill the frame, subject crops the edges, no background
scale juxtaposition, towering figure vs tiny figure (power vs powerless)
visual weight balance, large quiet mass offset by small bright accent
rule of odds, three/five subjects (odd number) grouped
repetition and rhythmic pattern of identical elements
pattern with one odd-one-out element breaking the grid
low horizon (vast sky·grandeur) vs high horizon (oppressive·grounded)
dead-center frontal framing, subject staring into lens
